title: Baked fish and chips - Canada.ca
content:
## Baked fish and chips
This tasty dish is a great way to enjoy fish! Crushed almonds give a perfect, nutty crunch and the oven fries are irresistible. Serve with a green salad or coleslaw.
- 1 sweet potato, cut into strips
- 2 parsnips, cut into strips
- 5 mL (1 tsp) vegetable oil
- 2 mL (½ tsp) chili powder

### Almond Crusted Fish:
- 125 mL (½ cup) whole almonds
- 180 mL (¾ cup) whole grain breadcrumbs
- 10 mL (2 tsp) dried oregano
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) water
- 60 mL (¼ cup) whole wheat flour
- 10 mL (2 tsp) paprika
- 454 g (1 lb) tilapia fillets
- 2 mL (½ tsp) salt (optional)
- 2 mL (½ tsp) black pepper
- 1 lemon, cut into wedges
- Preheat the oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper. Set aside.
- Prepare oven fries: Combine sweet potato and parsnip strips in a large bowl. Add oil and chili powder. Toss to coat evenly and spread in a single layer onto 1 prepared baking sheet.
- Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until potatoes and parsnips are tender.
- For the fish, place almonds in the middle of a clean kitchen towel and wrap into a bundle. Using a meat mallet or rolling pin, crush almonds until finely crushed.
- Pour almonds into a large plate or tray and mix with whole grain breadcrumbs and oregano. Set aside.
- In another plate, create egg wash by whisking together egg with water. Set aside.
- In a third plate, mix flour together with paprika and set aside.
- Cut each fish fillet in half and season with salt (optional) and black pepper.
- To bread the fish, coat with flour and shake off any excess. Dip into egg wash and place into seasoned almond and breadcrumb mixture. Press down gently to adhere crumb coating.
- Place fish onto the second prepared baking sheet and bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden brown. Use a digital food thermometer to check that fish has reached an internal temperature of 70 °C (158 °F).
- Serve with lemon wedge.
Recipe developed by the Food Innovation and Research Studio at George Brown College for the Guelph Family Health Study’s cookbook and Health Canada.
Learn about food safety .
Crush almonds in to a fine crumb.
Prepare breading station.
Make sure fish is well coated before baking.
- Use your family’s favourite fresh or frozen fish. Shop around for the best deal!
- Bake a double batch of oven fries and freeze the extras in a single layer on a baking sheet. Use the next time you need a hearty side dish.
- Mix it up! Swap almonds for walnuts or sunflower seeds, or use bran cereal instead of breadcrumbs.
- Have little chefs whisk eggs or mix the flour and paprika.
